# Permutations

## Topic-Specific Variants

- One coupled operation that makes independently solved cycles interact.
- Many equal-length cycles where a canonical representative choice matters.

## Strong Adversarial Solutions

- The solver solves cycles independently even though the operation couples them.
- The solver depends on label order rather than permutation structure.

## Killer Constructions

- Hold cycle lengths fixed and move labels across positions.
- Embed one interacting cycle pair inside otherwise neutral cycles.
- Duplicate equal cycle lengths so tie-breaking between cycles becomes observable.
